title: MBLEx D1 Anatomy & Physiology v3 (clear stems + aligned distractors)

questions.prompt: Which structure is commonly called the windpipe?
questions.why: The trachea carries air from the larynx toward the bronchi.

questions.prompt: What causes a skeletal muscle fiber to relax after contraction?

choices.B: Calcium is pumped back into the sarcoplasmic reticulum
choices.D: ATP is pumped into the SR

questions.answer: Calcium is pumped back into the sarcoplasmic reticulum
questions.why: Relaxation occurs when calcium is removed from the cytosol and cross-bridge cycling stops. This links structure and function using standard physiology.
questions.trap: ATP is required, but relaxation depends on calcium reuptake into the SR.

questions.prompt: What causes a skeletal muscle fiber to relax after contraction? (choose the best answer)

questions.trap: ATP is required, but relaxation depends on calcium reuptake into the SR.

questions.prompt: Which process moves oxygen from alveoli into pulmonary capillaries?
